title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Root Cause & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour traces the full lifecycle of FIX-467, a bug where creating the first library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours. The root cause was that the system was incorrectly using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple agent runs were required to surface and fully resolve this issue. The tour follows the investigation in data-flow order: from the initial user report, through agent triage runs, to the final corrected token-selection logic and verification.

steps.description: This is the entry point: the original ticket FIX-467 filed by the user. The user reported that creating a new library ticket in the staging environment produced no artifacts after several hours of waiting. Screenshots were attached as evidence of the failure. A critical constraint is noted here — the agent's available database is not an exact copy of production, but the fix must ensure the new library is created regardless. This ticket context file seeds the entire investigation and sets the scope: the problem is environment-specific (staging) and involves the artifact creation pipeline.
steps.title: Step 1: Original Ticket Report (FIX-467)

steps.description: In this second run, the Helix agent acknowledged the ticket and began triage. The discussion section records the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run focused on understanding the failure mode from attached screenshots and available database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent acknowledgment → environment inspection. The agent is scoping the problem before touching any code.
steps.title: Step 2: Agent Acknowledges and Begins Investigation

steps.description: This run produced the first attempted fix, which the agent believed resolved the issue — verification screenshots were attached and the agent posted 'Your changes are ready! Updated 1 repository.' However, the user (Usher) replied at 2026-05-15T03:30:52Z confirming the problem persisted. The user's follow-up message is the key insight: 'It shouldn't be using the user's token. It should be using the built-in Helix token that creates repositories.' This clarified that the earlier fix addressed a surface symptom but missed the actual root cause: incorrect token selection for NetSuite file cabinet repo creation.
steps.title: Step 3: First Fix Attempt — Surface Symptom Addressed, Root Cause Missed

steps.description: This run captures the agent's deeper diagnosis after the user's clarification. The agent confirmed it understood the actual root cause: when creating repositories for NetSuite file cabinet repos, the code was propagating the user's OAuth token instead of switching to the built-in Helix system token. This is the pivotal run where the real fix was scoped — the token-selection logic needed to be corrected so that repository creation always uses the Helix system credential, not the initiating user's credential.
steps.title: Step 4: Root Cause Identified — Wrong Token Used for Repo Creation

steps.description: This is the intraview walkthrough generated during the run that identified the root cause. It documents the investigation trail in CodeTour format, providing a structured record of how the agent reasoned through the token-selection bug. This artifact serves both as documentation and as a verification checkpoint — reviewers can follow this tour to confirm the agent's understanding of the problem matched the actual fix applied.
steps.title: Step 5: Intraview Walkthrough — Investigation Trail Documented

steps.description: An updated intraview walkthrough from a subsequent run refines the documentation with additional clarity on the data-flow order: user report → agent triage → incorrect token identified → token-selection logic corrected → verification. The description here explicitly calls out that the system must use the Helix system token (not the user token) for NetSuite file cabinet repo creation. This walkthrough version adds more precise step titles and expands the explanation of why earlier runs missed the root cause.
steps.title: Step 6: Refined Intraview — Full Data-Flow Order Captured

steps.description: The final run's ticket context confirms the complete discussion history, including all agent responses, user follow-ups, and the final resolution. This file is the canonical record of the fix lifecycle. At this point, the token-selection logic has been corrected: repository creation for NetSuite file cabinet repos now routes through the Helix system credential rather than the user's OAuth token, ensuring artifact creation succeeds regardless of the initiating user's permissions.
steps.title: Step 7: Final Run — Fix Confirmed and Ticket Closed

steps.description: This is the final intraview walkthrough generated after the complete fix was applied and verified. It is the most complete version of the CodeTour, covering all steps from initial report through root cause identification to the corrected token-selection logic. The tour confirms that all major code paths remain clean: (1) the artifact creation pipeline now correctly uses the Helix system token for repo creation, (2) the staging environment constraint (non-exact database copy) is handled gracefully, and (3) the fix is scoped correctly to NetSuite file cabinet repos without regressing other repo types.
steps.title: Step 8: Summary — Final Walkthrough Confirms All Code Paths Clean
